blockingIssues.description: Qualified imported types are not parsed in type position, so contracted cases like `http::Request` fail at parse time instead of reaching the import-aware diagnostics required by VAL-IMPORT-004.
Imported aliases are not fully reserved at file scope because traits named `http` bypass collision checks, so `use net::http; trait http { ... }` succeeds instead of reporting an explicit collision.
Codegen hardcodes `http` as an imported package alias, so local `http::...` paths are mis-lowered as `net/http` even when no `use net::http` import exists.
Imported stdlib types are incorrectly accepted in expression position; `http::Request` can typecheck as a value and then lowers to invalid Go (`http.Request` used as an expression).

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify the tests-first requirement and/or the meaning of `followedProcedure` in handoff feedback so scrutiny reviewers can treat that field as reliable evidence.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The review for `import-use-syntax-and-import-diagnostics` found the worker edited production files before tests, but the handoff still reported `followedProcedure: true` despite the skill stating tests should be written first.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Require a regression where a local declaration shares a stdlib alias without importing it, so naming-bridge work proves lowering is driven by resolved import state rather than raw identifier text.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The review for `net-http-namespace-and-naming-bridge` found `lib/codegen.ml` hardcodes `http` as stdlib; a local `enum http { Ok }` case was not covered by the feature's regression suite.
